stem: The height, in feet, of an object x seconds after it is thrown straight up in the air can be modeled by the function h(x) = -16x2 + 28x + 4. Based on the model, which of the following statements best interprets the equation h(0.8) = 16.16?

answerOptions.content: The height of the object 16.16 seconds after being thrown straight up in the air is 0.8 feet.
The speed of the object 0.8 seconds after being thrown straight up in the air is approximately 16.16 feet per second.
The height of the object 0.8 seconds after being thrown straight up in the air is 16.16 feet.
The height of the object 16.16 seconds after being thrown straight up in the air is approximately 0.8 times its initial height.

rationale: Choice C is correct. The variable x is the number of seconds after the object was thrown, and h(x) is the height in feet. The equation h(0.8) = 16.16 therefore says that 0.8 seconds after the object was thrown, its height is 16.16 feet. Choice A swaps the meanings of time and height. Choice B describes a speed, not a height. Choice D compares to initial height incorrectly.
